name: Karelian
notes: Karelian (karjala), a Finnic (Uralic) language of the Republic of Karelia and Tver Oblast, Russia. Its unified Latin orthography extends the Finnish letter set with ⟨š⟩ /ʃ/, ⟨ž⟩ /ʒ/, ⟨č⟩ /t͡ʃ/, the digraph ⟨dž⟩ /d͡ʒ/ and ⟨z⟩ /z/, ⟨c⟩ /t͡s/; ⟨v⟩ is the labial approximant /ʋ/ (inherited from the Finnish base). Vowel letters a e i o u y ä ö, doubled for phonemic length; consonant length is written by doubling. Karelian has an extensive phonemic palatalised consonant series (/tʲ dʲ nʲ lʲ sʲ rʲ zʲ/) marked in narrow transcription with ⟨ʲ⟩ and, in the orthography, chiefly conditioned before front vowels and ⟨i⟩; it is not resolved from the plain spelling here, so palatalisation is under-generated. Diphthong offglides are written plain. Inventory and doubled-length convention follow the Finnish base; the Finnic clade classification is shared.

sources.author: Wikipedia contributors
sources.title: Karelian language — Phonology and Orthography
sources.notes: Unified Karelian Latin alphabet (adds š ž č dž z c), consonant/vowel quantity, and the phonemic palatalised consonant series.

ancestors.notes: Finnic — the classification node this language sits under.
